title: Tsunami self-evacuation after strong coastal shaking
summary: After strong or long coastal shaking, move away from the shore and to higher ground without waiting to watch the sea.
content:
After strong or long coastal shaking, move away from the shore and to higher ground without waiting to watch the sea.

Action sequence: Protect yourself during the earthquake, then leave the coastal zone as soon as shaking stops and movement is safe. Use the nearest route inland or to higher ground; travel on foot when roads are blocked or authorities direct it and the route itself is safe. Stay away from beaches, harbours, river mouths, low coastal roads, and viewpoints. Remain in the safe area until competent authorities announce the danger has ended because multiple waves may arrive. Safety boundary: Do not go to the shore to watch or photograph the sea. Do not return after the first wave or when the water appears calm.
materials: closed shoes. Context: “Tsunami self-evacuation after strong coastal shaking”.
steps: Protect yourself during the earthquake, then leave the coastal zone as soon as shaking stops and movement is safe.

Stay away from beaches, harbours, river mouths, low coastal roads, and viewpoints.
Remain in the safe area until competent authorities announce the danger has ended because multiple waves may arrive.
warnings: Do not go to the shore to watch or photograph the sea.
Do not return after the first wave or when the water appears calm.

Do not continue when the scene exposes people to fire, smoke, floodwater, live electricity, gas, violence, unstable structures, or another uncontrolled hazard. title: Island ferry disruption household continuity
summary: Prepare for delayed medicines, food, fuel, mail, and travel by tracking essential run-out dates and local alternatives.
content:
Prepare for delayed medicines, food, fuel, mail, and travel by tracking essential run-out dates and local alternatives.

Action sequence: List medicines, medical consumables, infant needs, animal feed, water, staple food, fuel, and critical work supplies with run-out dates. Check official port, ferry, weather, municipality, pharmacy, and utility information rather than travel rumours. Contact prescribers and suppliers early for critical items and identify local substitutes that professionals approve. Reduce nonessential consumption before the final reserve is reached and protect a minimum emergency quantity. Safety boundary: Do not ration prescription medicine or substitute products without professional advice. Do not make a dangerous sea or road journey merely to avoid inconvenience.

title: Village wildfire early-departure trigger
summary: Set earlier departure conditions for villages with one road, steep terrain, dense vegetation, animals, older residents, or slow transport.
content:
Set earlier departure conditions for villages with one road, steep terrain, dense vegetation, animals, older residents, or slow transport.

Action sequence: Map every exit, turn-around point, narrow section, bridge, fuel area, vegetation corridor, and place where traffic may block. Identify households needing transport, mobility help, medicine continuity, animal carriers, or livestock decisions. Use official warnings, local fire conditions, wind, smoke, visibility, road status, and transport time to set a written early-departure threshold. When the threshold or official order is reached, leave by the designated route and do not wait to see flames. Safety boundary: Do not block the only road while loading property or unmanaged animals. Do not send residents toward the fire to gather information.

Heatwave room-by-room cooling plan without air conditioning

title: Heatwave room-by-room cooling plan without air conditioning
summary: Choose the coolest usable room, control sun and heat entry, schedule ventilation, and relocate vulnerable people before indoor heat becomes dangerous.
content:
Choose the coolest usable room, control sun and heat entry, schedule ventilation, and relocate vulnerable people before indoor heat becomes dangerous.

Action sequence: Measure rooms at different times and select the coolest safe sleeping and daytime area. Close external shading and sun-facing openings during peak heat when outside air is hotter; ventilate when outside conditions become cooler and safe. Reduce cooking, appliances, physical work, and crowding; schedule water, checks, and cool-location visits for vulnerable people. Set symptoms, indoor conditions, or power loss that trigger movement to an air-conditioned public or trusted location and call for medical help when needed. Safety boundary: Do not rely on a fan alone when the room remains dangerously hot or a vulnerable person is worsening. Do not leave medicines, batteries, children, dependent adults, or animals in the hottest room or a parked vehicle.
steps: Measure rooms at different times and select the coolest safe sleeping and daytime area.
Close external shading and sun-facing openings during peak heat when outside air is hotter; ventilate when outside conditions become cooler and safe.
Reduce cooking, appliances, physical work, and crowding; schedule water, checks, and cool-location visits for vulnerable people.
Set symptoms, indoor conditions, or power loss that trigger movement to an air-conditioned public or trusted location and call for medical help when needed.
warnings: Do not rely on a fan alone when the room remains dangerously hot or a vulnerable person is worsening.
Do not leave medicines, batteries, children, dependent adults, or animals in the hottest room or a parked vehicle.

title: Long water-cut apartment allocation plan
summary: Separate drinking, medicine, food, hand hygiene, toilet, cleaning, and animal needs so limited safe water is not consumed invisibly.
content:
Separate drinking, medicine, food, hand hygiene, toilet, cleaning, and animal needs so limited safe water is not consumed invisibly.

Action sequence: Confirm whether the event is a supply interruption, boil-water notice, or do-not-use notice because each requires different action. Measure available verified drinking water and list people, medical needs, infants, older adults, animals, and the expected duration. Reserve safe water first for drinking and medically necessary preparation, then assign separate sources for other uses. Record every refill source, treatment, container, date, issue, remaining quantity, and next review. Safety boundary: Do not use a boil-water method when authorities issue a do-not-use notice for chemical or other non-microbial contamination. Do not store drinking water in containers that held fuel, pesticides, solvents, or cleaners.
